items.title: Self-Hosting AI Models After Claude's Usage Limits
items.summary: After Claude Pro changed to weekly limits, I explored self-hosting Qwen3-Coder-480B with 400k context windows. Here's what I learned about costs, alternatives, and why Claude Code still dominates the landscape.

items.title: Logging Privacy Shenanigans
items.summary: Apple's logs redact your debugging data as <private>. Here's what actually gets hidden, why old tricks don't work anymore, and the only reliable way to see your logs again.

items.title: VibeTunnel's first AI-anniversary
items.summary: It's been one month since we released the first version of VibeTunnel, and since in the AI world time is so much faster, let's call it VibeTunnel's first anniversary!

items.title: Making AppleScript Work in macOS CLI Tools: The Undocumented Parts
items.summary: How to make AppleScript work in macOS CLI tools without permission dialogs blaming Terminal. A deep dive into Info.plist embedding, TCC, and undocumented APIs born from building Terminator MCP.

items.title: Peekaboo 2.0 – Free the CLI from its MCP shackles
items.summary: Peekaboo 2.0 ditches the MCP-only approach for a CLI-first architecture, because CLIs are the universal interface that both humans and AI agents can actually use effectively

items.summary: Enhance your Claude Code workflow with VibeTunnel terminal title management for better multi-session tracking
